seasonality: Outdoor park time is easier to extend in mild weather.
Cold or rainy days should bias the schedule toward museums and shorter outdoor walks.
transport_advice: Museum Mile and Central Park can be paired in the same day because they are geographically close.
Use the subway or short rideshare transfers instead of crossing boroughs between museum stops.
planning_tips: For a museum-heavy New York day, avoid scheduling more than two major museums unless the user explicitly asks for an intense pace.
Pair The Metropolitan Museum of Art with Central Park or nearby Upper East Side food stops to reduce backtracking.
Keep evening dining near the final sightseeing area or a direct subway line back to the hotel.
title: New York Museum Mile and Central Park Planning Notes
content: NYC Tourism presents New York City as a five-borough destination with major categories such as museums, galleries, tours, attractions, neighborhoods, dining, and parks. For itinerary planning, Manhattan museum clusters and Central Park work well as a same-day grouping because they reduce cross-city transfers and support a realistic public-transit pace.

seasonality: Summer citywalk days should include indoor breaks and hydration.
Winter itineraries should shorten outdoor bridge or waterfront walks.
transport_advice: Lower Manhattan, Chinatown, SoHo, and the Brooklyn Bridge area are better handled as a clustered downtown route.
Avoid pairing downtown history stops with far north Manhattan attractions on the same short day.
planning_tips: Use downtown history stops in the morning, then place lunch around Chinatown, SoHo, or nearby neighborhoods.
If the user asks for food and walking, prioritize compact neighborhoods over scattered landmark hopping.
Keep the Brooklyn Bridge walk optional when weather or pacing is unfavorable.
title: New York Downtown History, Food, and Citywalk Planning Notes
content: New York planning benefits from grouping attractions by neighborhood because the city has dense but distinct areas. A downtown route can connect history, citywalk, and food experiences while avoiding unnecessary transfers between Lower Manhattan and uptown museum districts.
